system_description.overview: Portant is an AI-powered document automation platform that helps businesses streamline the creation of quotes, proposals, contracts, and other sales documents. It integrates with HubSpot, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365 to generate personalized, compliant, and branded documents automatically. The platform supports complex workflows, e-signatures, dynamic templates, and CRM data merging.

products.Document Automation: Automated generation of quotes, proposals, contracts and other business documents using dynamic templates (Google Docs, Slides, Microsoft Word/PowerPoint)
products.Workflow Automation: Customisable document workflows including approval processes and e-signature requests, audit trails, access control, and document versioning
products.Platform Integrations: Seamless integrations with HubSpot, Google Workspace, and Microsoft 365; webhooks and API capabilities
products.HubSpot CRM Integration: Natively integrated into HubSpot's ecosystem to provide seamless proposal, quote and e-signature workflow

regions: Not specifically named; GCP multi-AZ described
infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; no physical servers
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (spins up multiple servers across all availability zones in GCP on failover)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in GCP Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) with segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Customer accounts are appropriately segmented from each other.
network_architecture.api_gateway: API Instance Group (Compute Engine auto-scaling) serving all REST API requests at api.portant.co
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols and services; reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via GCP to identify and protect against threats
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access via bastion host with VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS using certificates from an established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; email scanning to prevent malicious scripts
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> GCP VPC: API Instance Group (Compute Engine, auto-scaling, serves api.portant.co) -> Services Instance -> Pub/Sub -> Automation Runners (Cloud Run). Admin Instance at admin.portant.co. DB: Cloud SQL.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested before migration to production

file_storage: Not specifically named; GCP storage implied
data_storage.backup_frequency: Not specified (backup policy exists; backup and restoration logs maintained for defined retention periods)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: GCP (passwords controlled through GCP Password policy); Active Directory mentioned for server/database accounts
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required to access source code tool; 2FA for employee email
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: GCP password policy with periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements; account lockout after failed attempts; no guest/anonymous logins

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: GCP KMS (cloud provider key management service); access to production environment's access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA access; branch rules configured)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to require additional approval for every merge request to move change to production environment
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback (baseline configurations retained as code in infrastructure)
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management set up by CTO; monthly patch management reviews; workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tools for capacity/anomaly detection)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: GCP logging (implied); logging enabled for administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions, security configurations changes
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for high and critical risk events; resolved via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure and data center services
CRM integration — data merging into documents, e-signature workflows

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and Malware protection software on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; definitions updated weekly); also on Mac workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — Physical access to data center; GCP is responsible for physical security
A1.2 — Environmental protections and software/data backup recovery infrastructure; managed by GCP/building management
notable_controls: CC8.1.2: Branch protection rules require additional approval for all merge requests to production
CC8.1.3: Source code access restricted to authorized users with MFA
CC6.1.1: MFA enforced for all administrative access to production platform
CC6.2.2: Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged
CC6.1.4: Access revoked within one business day of termination
CC4.1.1: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC7.4.1: BCDR policy tested annually per defined testing schedule

infrastructure.rationale: GCP with multi-AZ failover, VPC, Cloud Run serverless, IaC (config-as-code). No CDN mentioned, no multi-region, no named IaC tool (Terraform etc.), single cloud provider.

application_architecture.rationale: REST API with auto-scaling Compute Engine + Cloud Run workers. Separate environments, branch protection, code reviews. No languages/frameworks disclosed. Architecture is relatively simple for a document automation tool.

data_layer.rationale: Single database type (Cloud SQL), no caching layer mentioned, no data warehouse, no file storage named explicitly. Backup policy exists but frequency not disclosed. Per-tenant segregation confirmed.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen testing, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS via GCP, firewalls, MFA for admin access, antivirus on servers and endpoints, quarterly access reviews, 1-day access revocation SLA. No WAF specifically named, no SIEM named, no cyber insurance mentioned.

devops_maturity.rationale: Branch protection, separate environments, emergency change process, config-as-code (unnamed tool), automatic patch management. CI/CD tool not named, IaC tool not named. Source code tool not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, reviewed and tested annually, multi-AZ failover via GCP. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover, backup frequency not specified.

vendor_diversity.rationale: 5 identified vendors (GCP, HubSpot, Google Workspace, Microsoft 365, Google Mail). Heavy Google dependency (GCP + Workspace + Mail). Business model deeply tied to HubSpot and Google ecosystem.

overall.rationale: Solid security posture for a SOC 2 Type 1 report. GCP-native architecture with good access controls, annual pen testing, and BCDR testing. Weaknesses: minimal tech stack disclosure, no RTO/RPO, heavy Google dependency, no CDN or WAF named, CI/CD tooling opaque.

red_flags: No RTO or RPO disclosed — BCDR maturity cannot be fully assessed
No CDN or WAF explicitly named — DDoS and web application attack surface unclear
No SIEM tool named — security event correlation capability unknown
CI/CD tool, IaC tool, and source control tool names not disclosed — tooling quality unverifiable
yellow_flags: Single cloud provider (GCP) with no multi-region failover — regional outage would impact service
Heavy dependency on Google ecosystem (GCP + Google Workspace + Gmail) creates correlated risk
No caching layer disclosed — potential performance/availability concern at scale
Backup frequency not specified — data recovery exposure unknown
No data warehouse — analytics capability may be limited
green_flags: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
MFA enforced for all administrative access and source code management
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Access revocation within one business day of termination
BCDR plan tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
Branch protection with mandatory approval for all production merges
Antivirus on both production servers (weekly definition updates) and endpoints
key_observations: Portant is an Australian company (Sydney) operating entirely on GCP with no physical servers — all physical security delegated to GCP (carve-out method)

The network diagram reveals a distinctive architecture: auto-scaling Compute Engine instance groups for API serving, with a Services Instance feeding a Pub/Sub queue driving Cloud Run automation workers — well-suited for async document generation at scale
The platform's value proposition is deeply tied to Google and Microsoft ecosystems (integrations are core product features), creating both a business moat and a concentration risk
Security criteria only (no Availability, Confidentiality, Processing Integrity, or Privacy trust criteria) — a narrower scope than many peers

CC6.4 (physical access) and A1.2 (environmental/recovery infrastructure) are excluded because GCP owns these — standard for cloud-native carve-out but means no direct evidence of physical security controls
Source code tool has MFA-gated access but the tool itself (GitHub, GitLab, etc.) is not named — worth asking in due diligence
